titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 623

div.p: Alexander, by the grace of God king of Scots, to all worthy men of his whole land, clerk and lay, greeting: Let present and future know that we have granted and by this our charter to have made firm the donation which David of Quixwood made to God and to Saint Cuthbert and to Saint Æbbe, and to the monks of Durham at Coldingham, to those serving now and in perpetuity to those who shall serve in future, of his whole land which he had within and outwith the toun of Aldcambus, for the exchange of sixty acres in the territory of Coldingham, and one toft and croft which Waldef Pethun held and ten acres of arable land at Fleurs, and one messuage near the smiddy of Benedict the Smith. Wherefore we will, that the aforesaid monks aforementioned have and hold the land, as freely and quietly, as the charter of the aforesaid David and the confirmation of Thomas the son of the same David, made thereupon for the aforesaid monks declares, saving our service. As witnesses, Henry de Balliol, Chamberlain, Patrick son of Earl Patrick, Ralph the Chaplain, Thomas of Stirling the Clerk, Henry of Stirling, son of Earl David, John of Maxwell, Walter Comyn. At Selkirk, on the 9th day of December, in the Year of the Reign of the lord King, the Tenth.
